RO Reject

Cooling Tower Blowdown Reuse: Step-by-Step Industrial Roadmap

Home/Blog/Article

Blowdown reuse requires chemistry discipline, not only hardware

Cooling tower blowdown can be a valuable reuse source when chemistry is controlled well. Reuse projects fail most often due to scaling and corrosion assumptions, not pump sizing.

Begin with seasonal water chemistry profiling and concentration cycle analysis. This defines realistic recovery limits before treatment.

Typical treatment combinations include clarification, filtration, and membrane concentration depending on silica, hardness, and chloride levels.

Protect reuse reliability with antiscalant optimization and robust pretreatment. Small pretreatment mistakes multiply quickly in membrane sections.

Decide reuse destinations by quality requirement: utility wash, process makeup, or high-grade loops. Matching quality to use-case improves recovery economics.

Track corrosion indices and membrane differential pressure weekly. These two indicators provide early warnings before visible performance loss.
